Application Examples

These case studies show how the HAIF framework has been applied in real hospital settings. Each example walks through the four phases — Exploration, Preparation, Implementation, and Sustainment — as applied to a specific clinical context.

Example 1: Acupressure for PONV

Nurse-led acupressure wristband implementation for post-operative nausea and vomiting at Northern Hospital, Victoria. A completed case study demonstrating all four phases.

View case study →

Example 2: Acupuncture in Emergency Department Coming Soon

Registrar-led acupuncture implementation in the emergency department. Research is currently in progress.

Have an Implementation Story? If you have implemented acupuncture or acupressure in a hospital setting and would like your experience featured as a case study, contact us.

Last reviewed: April 2026

Hospital Acupuncture Implementation Framework (HAIF)
Developed by Dr Zhen Zheng, PhD · RMIT University
NHMRC Translating Research into Practice Fellow
